caption:
The lecture hall hummed with the electric anticipation of a thousand curious minds, but all eyes were glued to the front, not to the blackboard, but to me. I adjusted my silk blouse, feeling the cool fabric slide against my skin as a wave of heat rolled through the room. Today, I wasn't just a professor of Art History; I was a vision of desire, draped in a figure-hugging black pencil skirt that hugged my curves, a tight white camisole that barely concealed the sway of my assets beneath. The air smelled of old paper and vanilla perfume. Professor Elena Rossi, from the sun-kissed hills of Italy, had a way of turning a simple morning lecture into a private symphony of seduction. I watched a young, eager student fidget in the back row, his eyes darting between the Renaissance masterpieces on my desk and the way my skirt rode up slightly as I reached for a sketchbook. I smiled, a slow, knowing curve that made the room blush, and whispered, 'Shall we begin with something closer to your heart?'
